overview:
**High fire danger continues today, driven by warm, dry fuels (ERC 80%, BI 66%) despite calm winds.**
- Adjective fire danger is forecast to stay High through July 30, with ERC/BI easing slightly by midweek as rain chances rise into next week.
- Winds stay light (NE 5-7 mph), with a 30% chance of afternoon showers/storms today and highs near 85; no Red Flag Warning is in effect.
- Local watchout status: 0 of 7 thresholds met.
- Grand Mesa, Uncompahgre and Gunnison National Forests are under Stage 2 Fire Restrictions; BLM Gunnison Field Office has Stage 1 restrictions in Gunnison, Hinsdale, Montrose, Ouray, Saguache, and San Juan counties.
- Nearby: Gold Mountain Fire near Ouray is 38,044 acres, 24% contained; Elk Fire, 7 miles northwest of Lake City, is 2,307 acres and 0% contained.
lead: High fire danger continues today, driven by warm, dry fuels (ERC 80%, BI 66%) despite calm winds.
